whatItDoes: Decodes URL shortener and safelink redirect URLs to reveal the final destination address. Supports batch decoding of multiple URLs at once and handles common redirect patterns including query parameter extraction, path-based redirects, and base64-encoded destinations.
whyItExists: URL shorteners and safelink services hide the true destination of a link, which can obscure malicious sites. This tool lets you verify where a link actually goes before clicking it, improving online safety and awareness.
whoShouldUse: Security-conscious users, IT administrators verifying link destinations, content creators checking shared links, and anyone who receives shortened URLs and wants to know the real destination.
useCases: Verify the real destination of a shortened URL before clicking on it
Batch-decode multiple safelinks from an email or document
Check that a shared link leads to a legitimate and safe website
Extract the original URL from a link obfuscation service
Decode redirect chains from tracking or affiliate URLs
instructions: Paste one or more safelink or shortened URLs into the textarea, one per line
Click the "Decode URLs" button to process all links
Review the decoded destination URLs displayed below each original
Click "Copy" next to any decoded URL to copy it to your clipboard
Use "Copy All" to copy every decoded URL at once
examples: Input: https://safelink.example.com/?u=aHR0cHM6Ly9... -> Output: the decoded base64 URL
bestPractices: Always decode suspicious links before sharing them with others
Decode links received in unsolicited emails or messages before visiting
Use batch mode to check multiple links from a spreadsheet or document at once
Be aware that some redirects may chain through multiple URLs
Combine with other safety tools like URL reputation checkers for maximum security
commonMistakes: Assuming a decoded URL is safe just because it looks legitimate
Not decoding URLs before sharing them, potentially spreading malicious links
Expecting all shortened URLs to decode successfully — some use server-side redirects that cannot be decoded client-side
Pasting multiple URLs on the same line instead of one per line
faq: Can this tool decode all URL shorteners? No — server-side redirects (like bit.ly) do not embed the destination in the URL and require an HTTP request to resolve.
Is it safe to paste URLs here? Yes, the tool processes everything locally in your browser. No data is sent to any server.
What if the output looks like gibberish? The destination may be double-encoded or use a format not yet supported. Try URL-decoding the output once more.
